items.title: Anthropic models were pulled by export order
items.oneliner: Anthropic pulled Mythos and Fable worldwide after a U.S. order. Frontier model access now looks geopolitically fragile.

items.title: OpenAI faces a 42-state subpoena
items.oneliner: Forty-two states subpoenaed OpenAI over safety, ads, and minors. Consumer AI regulation just moved from talk to discovery.

items.title: Moonshot released a stronger coding model
items.oneliner: Moonshot's Kimi-K2.7-Code posts double-digit gains over its predecessor. Open coding models are still improving fast enough to matter.

items.title: OpenRouter fused multiple models into one
items.oneliner: OpenRouter Fusion merges parallel model outputs and claims near-Fable 5 quality for less. Routing is becoming a product.

items.title: Rio's flagship model faces provenance questions
items.oneliner: Investigators say Rio-3.5-Open-397B is a Nex-Qwen merge with a masking prompt. Model provenance is becoming a compliance problem.

items.title: curl paused vulnerability intake for July
items.oneliner: curl will stop accepting vulnerability reports for a month. Critical open source still depends on maintainers who can burn out.

items.title: Salesforce is buying Fin for $3.6 billion
items.oneliner: Salesforce is paying $3.6 billion for Fin. Customer support agents are consolidating into the CRM stack.

items.title: LangChain built a database for agent traces
items.oneliner: LangChain introduced SmithDB for agent observability. Trace storage is graduating from logs to purpose-built infrastructure.

items.title: Postgres deletes still do not scale
items.oneliner: PlanetScale argues the only scalable Postgres delete is DROP TABLE. MVCC costs are now a product architecture concern.

items.title: China cut 12,000 obsolete degree programs
items.oneliner: China's universities cut more than 12,000 degree programs for AI-era fields. Talent pipelines are being rewired at national scale.
